meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Miso-Ginger Shrimp + Lentil Bowl
meals.instructions: Whisk together miso paste, coconut aminos, sesame oil, grated ginger, and minced garlic into a sauce. Steam broccoli until just tender. In a hot skillet, toss the shrimp with half the sauce and stir-fry for 2 minutes until heated through. Assemble over lentils and edamame, add the broccoli, and drizzle remaining sauce over the top. Finish with sliced scallion.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Peruvian Aji Amarillo Chicken Bowl
meals.instructions: Rub chicken with cumin, salt, and a spoonful of aji amarillo paste. Sear in a hot pan over medium-high heat, 4–5 minutes per side, until cooked through. Let rest, then slice. Build the bowl over quinoa and black beans, add fresh spinach, and fan the chicken on top. Spoon remaining aji amarillo paste over the chicken, top with sliced avocado, a squeeze of lime, and fresh cilantro.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Tuna Avocado Lettuce Wraps
meals.instructions: Drain tuna and mash with avocado, a squeeze of lime, and hot sauce until combined. Spoon into romaine leaves and serve as wraps.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Indian Turkey Keema + Cauliflower Rice
meals.instructions: Sauté diced onion until softened, then add ginger, garlic, garam masala, and curry paste — cook 1 minute until fragrant. Add ground turkey and brown, breaking it up as it cooks. Stir in diced tomato and red lentils, simmer 5 minutes. Fold in spinach until wilted. Serve over cauliflower rice, cooked separately in a dry pan until lightly golden.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: BBQ Turkey + Roasted Sweet Potato + Broccoli
meals.instructions: Cube sweet potato and roast at 425°F with a pinch of salt until caramelized, about 25 minutes. Toss broccoli florets with a little oil and roast on the same pan for the last 15 minutes. Brush turkey breast with BBQ sauce and roast until cooked through, then slice. Plate everything together with sliced avocado on the side.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Turkey Roll-Ups + Hummus + Bell Pepper
meals.instructions: Spread hummus on turkey slices, roll them up tightly. Slice bell pepper into strips and serve alongside for dipping.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Thai Basil Shrimp + Cauliflower Rice
meals.instructions: Mix Thai chili paste, coconut aminos, fish sauce, and lime juice into a sauce. Stir-fry cauliflower rice in sesame oil until lightly golden, then set aside. In the same hot pan, cook sliced bell pepper and snap peas for 2 minutes, then add shrimp and edamame. Pour the sauce over everything and toss for 1–2 minutes until glossy and fragrant. Serve over cauliflower rice and finish with fresh Thai basil.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Mexican Turkey Burrito Bowl
meals.instructions: Season ground turkey with cumin, salt, and diced jalapeño. Cook in a hot pan, breaking it apart, until browned and cooked through. Warm the black beans separately. Build the bowl: cauliflower rice as the base, topped with turkey, black beans, and shredded romaine. Spoon salsa roja generously over everything, add guacamole, a squeeze of lime, and fresh cilantro.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Shrimp Cocktail + Guac + Veggie Sticks
meals.instructions: Arrange chilled shrimp on a plate with cocktail sauce for dipping and guacamole on the side. Serve with celery sticks and cucumber slices.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Korean Ground Turkey Bowl
meals.instructions: Stir gochujang, sesame oil, rice vinegar, and minced garlic and ginger together into a sauce. Brown ground turkey in a hot pan with a little of the sauce mixed in. Toss shredded cabbage and snap peas with a splash of rice vinegar. Layer brown rice in a bowl, top with turkey, black beans, edamame, and the cabbage slaw. Drizzle remaining sauce over everything and garnish with scallion.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Harissa Chicken + Sweet Potato + Chickpeas
meals.instructions: Rub chicken with half the harissa paste, cumin, and paprika. Let it marinate a few minutes while you prep the vegetables. Roast cubed sweet potato and chickpeas at 425°F for 20 minutes. Add sliced zucchini to the pan for the last 10 minutes. Meanwhile, sear the chicken in a hot pan 4–5 minutes per side until cooked through. Whisk tahini with lemon juice and a splash of water for a drizzle. Plate everything and finish with the tahini sauce and remaining harissa.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Chicken + Salsa Verde + Cucumber + Avocado
meals.instructions: Slice chicken and arrange on a plate. Spoon salsa verde over the chicken. Serve with cucumber slices and avocado on the side.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Mediterranean Chicken + Chickpea Salad
meals.instructions: Season chicken with cumin, oregano, salt, and pepper. Grill or sear over medium-high heat until golden and cooked through, about 5 minutes per side. Whisk tahini with lemon juice and a splash of water to make a dressing. Toss spinach, chickpeas, cucumber, and olives together, then drizzle with tahini dressing. Slice chicken and lay it over the salad, finishing with fresh parsley.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Korean Salmon + Shrimp Rice Bowl
meals.instructions: Mix gochujang, coconut aminos, and sesame oil into a glaze. Brush salmon with half the glaze and pan-sear skin-side down over medium-high heat for 4 minutes, then flip for 2 more minutes. Toss shrimp with remaining glaze and add to the pan for the last 2 minutes. Serve over brown rice with edamame, shredded cabbage, and sliced avocado alongside.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Egg + Egg White Salad in Endive Leaves
meals.instructions: Hard-boil eggs and egg whites, then chop and combine with mashed avocado, Dijon mustard, and lemon juice. Season with salt and pepper. Spoon into endive leaves and serve.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Chicken Tikka + Lentil Bowl
meals.instructions: Coat chicken in tikka masala paste and marinate for a few minutes. Cook in a hot pan over medium-high heat until charred at the edges and cooked through, about 5–6 minutes per side. Sauté onion, garlic, and ginger, then add diced tomato and simmer with red lentils for 8 minutes. Fold spinach in at the end. Serve the tikka chicken over the lentil base with cauliflower rice and fresh cilantro.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Tuna Niçoise Bowl
meals.instructions: Whisk together lemon juice, Dijon mustard, garlic, and capers into a bright vinaigrette. Blanch snap peas in boiling water for 1 minute, then drain. Arrange chickpeas, snap peas, tomato, cucumber, and olives in a bowl. Flake tuna over the top, add sliced avocado, and drizzle generously with the mustard vinaigrette. Finish with fresh herbs.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Turkey Lettuce Cups with Ginger Sauce
meals.instructions: Cook ground turkey with minced ginger and garlic in sesame oil until browned. Stir in coconut aminos and cook 1 more minute. Spoon into butter lettuce cups with shredded carrot and a sprinkle of scallion.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Japanese Shrimp Donburi
meals.instructions: Whisk miso paste, coconut aminos, and sesame oil into a savory glaze. Toss shrimp in the glaze and sear in a hot pan for 2 minutes. Wilt spinach briefly in the same pan. Build the bowl with a base of brown rice and green lentils, then layer the shrimp and wilted spinach on top. Add edamame, a few slices of pickled ginger, and scallion to finish.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: BBQ Chicken + Roasted Broccoli + Black Beans
meals.instructions: Dust chicken with garlic powder, onion powder, and smoked paprika. Grill or pan-sear over medium-high heat, brushing with BBQ sauce in the last 2 minutes of cooking. Roast broccoli at 425°F until crispy at the edges, about 20 minutes. Warm black beans in a small pot. Plate everything together with sliced avocado.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Tuna + Hummus + Romaine Wraps
meals.instructions: Mix drained tuna with a few dashes of hot sauce. Spread hummus on romaine leaves, add the tuna and sliced cucumber, and roll up to eat.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Turkish Turkey Kofta + Lentils + Tahini
meals.instructions: Mix ground turkey with cumin, coriander, paprika, garlic, and fresh parsley. Shape into small kofta logs or patties. Pan-fry over medium-high heat for 3–4 minutes per side until nicely charred and cooked through. Cook cauliflower rice in a dry pan until golden. Whisk tahini with lemon juice and a splash of water. Serve kofta over lentils and cauliflower rice with wilted spinach, drizzled with tahini sauce.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Sesame Ginger Chicken + Edamame + Snap Peas
meals.instructions: Whisk miso, coconut aminos, sesame oil, grated ginger, and garlic into a glaze. Slice chicken thin and stir-fry in a very hot pan for 4–5 minutes until caramelized. Remove chicken and stir-fry broccoli, snap peas, and edamame for 3 minutes. Return chicken to the pan, pour the glaze over everything, and toss. Serve over brown rice with sliced scallion and chili.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Shrimp Ceviche-Style with Avocado
meals.instructions: Toss shrimp with lime juice, diced tomato, cucumber, red onion, jalapeño, cilantro, and hot sauce. Let sit 5 minutes for the flavors to meld. Serve topped with cubed avocado.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Tandoori Turkey + Roasted Chickpeas + Cauliflower
meals.instructions: Toss chickpeas with tikka paste and roast at 425°F for 20 minutes until crispy. Meanwhile, mix ground turkey with cumin, turmeric, coriander, garlic, and a squeeze of lemon, then cook in a hot pan until browned and cooked through. Sauté cauliflower rice until golden. Wilt spinach in the same pan. Serve turkey and chickpeas over cauliflower rice, topped with wilted spinach.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Shrimp + Mango-Avocado Salsa + Cauliflower Rice
meals.instructions: Season shrimp with a pinch of salt and hot sauce, then sear in a hot pan for 2 minutes. Cook cauliflower rice in a separate dry pan until lightly golden. Dice mango, avocado, bell pepper, red onion, and jalapeño together; toss with lime juice and cilantro to make the salsa. Serve shrimp over cauliflower rice with black beans, topped generously with the mango-avocado salsa.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Chicken + Guacamole + Butter Lettuce Wraps
meals.instructions: Slice or shred chicken and spoon into butter lettuce cups. Top each wrap with guacamole and a spoonful of salsa roja.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Poke-Style Salmon + Tuna Bowl
meals.instructions: Whisk gochujang, coconut aminos, and sesame oil into a poke sauce. Cube salmon and toss with half the sauce; mix drained tuna with the remaining sauce. Arrange brown rice in a bowl, then fan the salmon and tuna side by side on top. Add edamame, sliced cucumber, and avocado around the bowl. Finish with scallion, sesame seeds, and pickled ginger.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Turkey + Harissa + Lentils + Zucchini
meals.instructions: Coat turkey breast in harissa paste with garlic, cumin, and smoked paprika; roast at 400°F until cooked through. Slice zucchini into half-moons and roast alongside the turkey for the last 15 minutes until caramelized. Whisk tahini with lemon juice for a drizzle. Serve sliced turkey over warm green lentils with roasted zucchini and wilted spinach, finished with tahini drizzle.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Egg White Scramble + Spinach + Avocado
meals.instructions: Sauté spinach with garlic and herbs in a non-stick pan until wilted. Pour in egg whites and scramble gently over medium heat until just set. Serve topped with sliced avocado and a few dashes of hot sauce.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Miso-Glazed Chicken + Bok Choy + Edamame
meals.instructions: Whisk miso paste, coconut aminos, sesame oil, grated ginger, minced garlic, and chili into a glaze. Coat chicken breast and marinate 10 minutes. Sear in a hot pan over medium-high heat, 5 minutes per side, basting with remaining glaze, until cooked through. In the same pan, stir-fry bok choy and broccoli for 3 minutes until vibrant. Serve over warm red lentils and edamame, with the chicken sliced on top.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Mexican Shrimp Fajita Bowl
meals.instructions: Toss shrimp with cumin, paprika, chili powder, and garlic. Sear in a hot cast-iron pan for 2 minutes until lightly charred. Roast sliced bell peppers at 425°F until caramelized. Cook cauliflower rice in a dry pan until golden. Build the bowl: cauliflower rice and black beans as the base, shrimp and peppers on top, finished with guacamole, salsa, and a squeeze of lime.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Turkey + Hummus + Endive Spears
meals.instructions: Spoon hummus into endive spears and top with sliced turkey. Arrange cucumber rounds alongside for extra crunch.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Chicken Shawarma + Cauliflower Rice + Chickpeas
meals.instructions: Coat chicken breast generously in shawarma spices and marinate briefly. Sear over medium-high heat, 5 minutes per side, until deeply golden and cooked through; rest then slice thin. Sauté cauliflower rice in a dry pan until lightly golden. Wilt spinach in the same pan. Whisk tahini with lemon juice and a splash of water into a drizzle. Build the bowl with cauliflower rice, chickpeas, and spinach, fan chicken on top, and finish with tahini drizzle and hot sauce.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Tuna + Lentil Salad + Dijon Vinaigrette
meals.instructions: Whisk Dijon mustard with lemon juice, minced garlic, and fresh herbs into a bright vinaigrette. Toss warm green lentils with the vinaigrette while still slightly warm so they absorb the flavor. Fold in diced tomato, cucumber, red onion, and olives. Drain tuna and flake over the top. Finish with sliced avocado.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Shrimp + Avocado + Salsa Verde Stack
meals.instructions: Smash avocado with lime juice and a pinch of salt. Layer romaine leaves with avocado, chilled shrimp, and a spoonful of salsa verde over the top.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Korean BBQ Chicken + Edamame + Quinoa
meals.instructions: Mix gochujang, coconut aminos, sesame oil, grated ginger, and garlic into a marinade. Coat chicken breast and marinate 15 minutes. Grill or sear over high heat, 5 minutes per side, until charred and cooked through; rest then slice. Toss shredded cabbage with a splash of coconut aminos. Serve chicken over quinoa and edamame with slaw on the side, scattered with sliced scallion and sesame seeds.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Salmon + Asparagus + Lentils + Tahini
meals.instructions: Season salmon with garlic, dill, and lemon zest. Roast broccoli and asparagus at 425°F until tender and caramelized, about 15 minutes. Sear salmon skin-side down in a hot pan for 4 minutes, flip, and cook 2 more minutes until just set in the center. Whisk tahini with lemon juice and a splash of water. Serve salmon over warm green lentils with the roasted vegetables and tahini drizzled over everything.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Tuna + Black Bean Dip + Veggie Sticks
meals.instructions: Mash black beans with cumin, garlic, lime juice, cilantro, and hot sauce into a chunky dip. Drain and flake tuna over the top. Serve with celery and cucumber sticks for scooping.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Turkey Larb-Inspired Bowl

ingredients.name: Lemongrass, shallots, mint, chili, toasted rice (GF)

meals.instructions: Dry-toast a pinch of rice in a pan until golden, then grind coarsely. Brown ground turkey in a hot wok over high heat, breaking it up. Mix lime juice, fish sauce, coconut aminos, sliced lemongrass, minced shallot, and chili into a dressing. Toss hot turkey with the dressing — it will sizzle and absorb. Fold in shredded cabbage and snap peas. Serve over green lentils, scattered with fresh mint and toasted rice.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Tom Yum-Inspired Shrimp + Cauliflower Rice

ingredients.name: Lemongrass, kaffir lime leaf, galangal, chili, coconut milk

meals.instructions: Simmer lemongrass, kaffir lime leaf, galangal, and chili in a small amount of water for 5 minutes to build a fragrant broth. Add coconut milk, fish sauce, coconut aminos, and lime juice. Stir-fry mushrooms and broccoli in a separate hot pan until tender. Add shrimp to the broth and warm through. Serve over cauliflower rice and black beans, with the broth ladled over the top.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Chicken + Avocado + Lime + Hot Sauce
meals.instructions: Slice or shred cooked chicken and toss with lime juice, hot sauce, red onion, cilantro, and diced jalapeño. Smash avocado roughly and season with salt. Serve chicken over romaine with avocado alongside.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Indian Egg + Lentil Masala
meals.instructions: Sauté diced onion until soft and golden. Add garlic, ginger, cumin, turmeric, coriander, and curry paste; cook until deeply fragrant, about 2 minutes. Stir in diced tomato and red lentils with a splash of water; simmer 5 minutes. Make wells in the sauce and crack in the whole eggs; cover and cook 4–5 minutes until whites are just set. Stir in egg whites and spinach during the last minute. Serve straight from the pan.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: BBQ Shrimp + Roasted Sweet Potato + Broccoli
meals.instructions: Cube sweet potato and roast at 425°F until caramelized and tender, about 25 minutes. Toss broccoli with garlic and paprika on the same sheet pan for the last 15 minutes. Toss shrimp in BBQ sauce and broil or sear in a hot pan for 2–3 minutes until sticky and slightly charred. Plate with roasted vegetables and sliced avocado on the side.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Turkey + Snap Peas + Ginger Sesame Dip
meals.instructions: Whisk coconut aminos, sesame oil, grated ginger, garlic, and a splash of rice vinegar into a dipping sauce. Serve with sliced turkey and crisp snap peas for dipping.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: GF Shrimp Pad Thai (Zucchini Noodles)

ingredients.name: GF tamarind paste, chili, garlic, scallion

meals.instructions: Whisk tamarind paste, coconut aminos, fish sauce, lime juice, and chili into a pad thai sauce. Stir-fry garlic and scallion in sesame oil over high heat for 30 seconds. Add shrimp, snap peas, and edamame; toss for 2 minutes. Add zucchini noodles and sauce; stir-fry 1–2 minutes until noodles are just tender and everything is coated. Fold in warm red lentils and serve immediately.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Chicken + Black Bean Enchilada Bowl

ingredients.name: Cumin, paprika, oregano, garlic, chili powder

meals.instructions: Season chicken breast with cumin, paprika, oregano, garlic, and chili powder. Sear in a hot pan over medium-high heat, 5 minutes per side, until cooked through; rest and shred. Simmer black beans in a spoonful of salsa roja until warmed through. Cook cauliflower rice in a dry pan until golden. Build the bowl with cauliflower rice, black beans, and shredded chicken, topped with remaining salsa roja, guacamole, and a squeeze of lime.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Tuna + Endive + Dijon Herb Dressing
meals.instructions: Whisk Dijon with lemon juice, chopped dill, parsley, and capers into a sharp herb dressing. Drain and flake tuna and toss with the dressing. Spoon into endive spears and top with sliced avocado.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Lemon-Herb Chicken + Asparagus + Chickpeas
meals.instructions: Rub chicken with minced garlic, rosemary, thyme, lemon zest, and salt. Roast at 400°F alongside asparagus and broccoli until cooked through, about 22 minutes. While the chicken rests, wilt spinach in the residual pan heat. Whisk tahini with lemon juice and a splash of water into a creamy drizzle. Plate over warm chickpeas with the roasted vegetables, finished with tahini.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Shrimp Taco Bowl (Dairy-Free)
meals.instructions: Toss shrimp with cumin, paprika, garlic, minced jalapeño, and lime juice. Sear in a very hot pan for 2 minutes until pink and lightly charred at the edges. Toss shredded cabbage with cilantro and a squeeze of lime. Cook cauliflower rice in a dry pan until golden. Layer the bowl: cauliflower rice and black beans, slaw, shrimp, then guacamole and salsa roja on top.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Mini Turkey Kofta + Dairy-Free Tzatziki
meals.instructions: Mix ground turkey with cumin, coriander, garlic, and salt. Shape into small oval patties and pan-fry over medium-high heat, 3 minutes per side, until cooked through and golden. Stir diced cucumber, dill, garlic, and lemon juice into coconut yogurt for a quick tzatziki. Serve kofta alongside with tzatziki for dipping.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Salmon Miso Ramen Bowl (GF Zucchini Noodles)
meals.instructions: Whisk miso paste, coconut aminos, and sesame oil into a hot cup of water for a broth. Sear salmon in a hot pan, skin-side down, 4 minutes; flip and cook 2 more minutes until just cooked through. In the same pan, stir-fry ginger, garlic, bok choy, and broccoli for 3 minutes. Add zucchini noodles and spinach and toss briefly. Pour miso broth over everything in the bowl, lay salmon on top, and finish with sliced scallion and chili.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Turkey-Stuffed Bell Peppers + Lentils
meals.instructions: Halve bell peppers and roast cut-side down at 400°F for 10 minutes until softened. Meanwhile, sauté diced onion and mushrooms until golden. Add garlic, cumin, paprika, and Italian herbs; cook 1 minute. Brown ground turkey in the same pan, then stir in tomato paste and green lentils with a splash of water. Simmer 5 minutes until thick and saucy. Fill the roasted pepper halves with the turkey-lentil mixture and return to the oven for 10 minutes.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Egg White Scramble + Avocado + Salsa
meals.instructions: Sauté spinach with garlic and chili flakes until wilted. Pour in egg whites and scramble gently over medium heat until just set and still soft. Serve topped with sliced avocado and salsa spooned over the top.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Chicken Souvlaki + Dairy-Free Tzatziki + Chickpeas
meals.instructions: Marinate chicken breast in lemon juice, oregano, rosemary, garlic, paprika, and olive oil for 15 minutes. Grill or sear over high heat, 5 minutes per side, until charred and cooked through; rest and slice. Stir cucumber, dill, and garlic into coconut yogurt for tzatziki. Serve sliced chicken over cauliflower rice and chickpeas with diced tomato and cucumber alongside, tzatziki spooned generously on top.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Garlic Herb Shrimp + Lentils + Greens

ingredients.name: Garlic, parsley, red chili flakes, white wine vinegar

meals.instructions: Roast broccoli at 425°F until crispy at the edges, about 15 minutes. Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat and sauté sliced garlic until golden. Add shrimp, chili flakes, lemon juice, and a splash of white wine vinegar; toss 2 minutes until heated through. Wilt spinach in the pan. Serve over warm green lentils with roasted broccoli, finished with fresh parsley.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Turkey Roll-Ups + Hummus + Celery
meals.instructions: Spread hummus on turkey slices and roll up tightly. Serve with celery sticks and bell pepper strips for dipping into any remaining hummus.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Turkish-Style Turkey + Eggplant + Lentils
meals.instructions: Cube eggplant, toss with olive oil and a pinch of baharat, and roast at 425°F until golden and silky, about 20 minutes. Sauté diced onion until deeply caramelized. Add ground turkey and remaining baharat; brown well. Stir in tomato paste, diced tomato, and green lentils with a splash of water; braise 5 minutes until thick and rich. Serve over cauliflower rice with roasted eggplant piled on top.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Tuna Chimichurri + Lentils + Roasted Peppers

ingredients.name: Chimichurri sauce (GF): parsley, cilantro, olive oil, garlic, vinegar

meals.instructions: Roast sliced bell peppers at 425°F until charred and tender, about 18 minutes. Blitz parsley, cilantro, garlic, olive oil, and red wine vinegar into a vibrant chimichurri. Toss warm green lentils with half the chimichurri. Drain tuna and fold through the lentils. Plate with wilted spinach and roasted peppers, top with sliced avocado, and spoon remaining chimichurri over everything with a squeeze of lime.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Shrimp + Endive + Tahini Lemon Dip
meals.instructions: Whisk tahini with lemon juice, minced garlic, cumin, and a splash of water until smooth and pourable. Arrange chilled shrimp in endive spears and drizzle tahini dip over the top. Garnish with fresh parsley.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Chicken Tikka + Cauliflower Rice + Lentils
meals.instructions: Coat chicken with tikka masala paste and let marinate 10 minutes. Sear in a hot pan over medium-high heat, 4–5 minutes per side, until cooked through. Rest and slice. Sauté cauliflower rice with garlic and ginger until just tender, then wilt in the spinach. Serve chicken over lentils and cauliflower rice, spooning any pan juices on top. Finish with fresh cilantro.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Korean Turkey + Black Beans + Edamame
meals.instructions: Whisk gochujang, coconut aminos, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger into a sauce. Brown turkey in a hot skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it up as it cooks. Pour half the sauce over the meat and stir to coat. Toss cabbage and edamame in a separate bowl with remaining sauce. Serve turkey over black beans, topped with the dressed cabbage slaw and sliced scallion.

meals.label: Meal 3
meals.name: Tuna + Avocado + Snap Peas + Quinoa
meals.instructions: Drain tuna and flake into a bowl. Mash avocado with lime juice and hot sauce, then fold into the tuna until loosely combined. Blanch snap peas in boiling water for 1 minute, then drain. Serve tuna mixture over quinoa with snap peas alongside.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Turkey Roll-Ups + Hummus + Celery
meals.instructions: Spread hummus over turkey slices and roll up. Serve alongside celery sticks for dipping in any remaining hummus.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Miso Shrimp + Edamame + Broccoli + Lentils
meals.instructions: Roast broccoli at 425°F for 18 minutes until charred at the edges. Whisk miso paste, coconut aminos, sesame oil, ginger, and garlic into a glaze. Toss shrimp in the glaze and heat in a skillet for 2 minutes until warmed through. Serve over lentils with edamame and roasted broccoli. Drizzle any remaining glaze over the bowl.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Mediterranean Chicken + Chickpeas + Spinach
meals.instructions: Season chicken with cumin, oregano, and salt. Sear in a hot pan 4–5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Whisk tahini with lemon juice and a splash of water until smooth. Build the bowl with spinach, chickpeas, and sliced cucumber. Slice chicken and arrange on top, then drizzle tahini dressing over everything. Finish with fresh parsley.

meals.label: Meal 3
meals.name: Egg + Lentil Indian Masala
meals.instructions: Sauté onion, garlic, and ginger with cumin until fragrant. Add curry paste and diced tomato, stirring for 2 minutes. Stir in lentils and spinach and let everything simmer together. Whisk whole eggs and egg whites, then pour over the lentil mixture. Fold gently and cook until just set — you want it creamy, not dry.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Shrimp + Guac + Romaine
meals.instructions: Toss chilled shrimp with lime juice. Spoon guacamole into romaine leaves and top with shrimp.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Thai Basil Shrimp + Cauliflower + Edamame
meals.instructions: Stir-fry bell pepper in a hot wok with sesame oil and garlic until slightly softened. Add coconut aminos, Thai chili paste, and fish sauce and let the sauce bubble for 30 seconds. Add shrimp and edamame and toss to coat. Serve over cauliflower rice, squeeze lime over the top, and pile on fresh Thai basil.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Harissa Turkey + Lentils + Zucchini + Spinach
meals.instructions: Rub turkey with harissa paste and roast or sear until cooked through. Slice zucchini and sauté with garlic and cumin until golden. Wilt spinach directly into the pan. Whisk tahini with lemon juice and a splash of water until pourable. Assemble lentils as the base, top with zucchini and spinach, then lay turkey slices on top and drizzle with tahini.

meals.label: Meal 3
meals.name: Peruvian Chicken + Quinoa + Black Beans
meals.instructions: Coat chicken in aji amarillo paste and sear in a hot skillet over medium-high heat, 4–5 minutes per side, until cooked through and slightly charred. Rest and slice. Build the bowl over quinoa and black beans, add fresh spinach, and fan the chicken on top. Finish with lime juice and cilantro.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Tuna + Avocado + Hot Sauce
meals.instructions: Drain tuna and mash with avocado, lime juice, and hot sauce. Serve in romaine leaves.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Salmon + Miso + Edamame + Greens
meals.instructions: Whisk miso paste, coconut aminos, sesame oil, ginger, and garlic into a glaze. Brush half over the salmon and roast at 400°F for 12–14 minutes until flaky. Roast broccoli alongside until charred. Wilt spinach in the warm pan. Serve salmon over edamame, spinach, and broccoli, then spoon remaining glaze over the top and finish with sliced scallion.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Turkey Tikka + Lentils + Cauliflower Rice
meals.instructions: Brown turkey with garlic, ginger, and coriander. Add tikka paste and stir to coat the meat, cooking for 1 minute. Stir in lentils and a splash of water, then wilt in spinach. Sauté cauliflower rice separately until fluffy. Serve turkey lentil mixture over cauliflower rice.

meals.label: Meal 3
meals.name: Shrimp Fajita Bowl + Black Beans
meals.instructions: Roast bell pepper strips at 425°F until softened and slightly charred. Season shrimp with cumin, paprika, and garlic, then sear in a hot skillet 1–2 minutes per side. Sauté cauliflower rice until fluffy. Build the bowl with cauliflower rice and black beans, top with shrimp and roasted peppers, spoon salsa roja over everything, and finish with lime and cilantro.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Chicken + Guac + Butter Lettuce
meals.instructions: Slice or shred cooked chicken. Spoon guacamole into butter lettuce cups, top with chicken, and add hot sauce to taste.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Korean Salmon + Shrimp + Edamame + Brown Rice
meals.instructions: Whisk gochujang, coconut aminos, and sesame oil into a sauce. Brush salmon with half the sauce and sear skin-side down in a hot pan for 4 minutes, then flip for 2 more. Toss shrimp in remaining sauce and warm in the same pan. Assemble over brown rice with edamame, shredded cabbage, and pickled ginger. Top with scallion and sesame seeds.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Mediterranean Tuna + Chickpeas + Greens
meals.instructions: Whisk tahini with lemon juice, garlic, and cumin until smooth. Drain tuna and flake into a large bowl with chickpeas, spinach, cucumber, and olives. Pour dressing over everything and toss gently. Finish with fresh herbs.

meals.label: Meal 3
meals.name: Turkey Kofta + Lentils + Tahini + Zucchini
meals.instructions: Mix turkey with cumin, coriander, garlic, and parsley. Shape into small patties or logs and pan-sear over medium-high heat until golden and cooked through. Roast zucchini at 425°F until golden. Whisk tahini with lemon juice and a splash of water until pourable. Plate lentils and zucchini, top with kofta, and drizzle tahini over everything.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Egg + Egg White + Endive + Dijon
meals.instructions: Scramble whole egg and egg whites together until just set. Spread a thin layer of Dijon on endive leaves, top with scrambled eggs, and add sliced avocado.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Indian Egg + Lentil Masala
meals.instructions: Cook garlic, ginger, cumin, and turmeric in a pan over medium heat until fragrant. Add curry paste and diced tomato and cook for 2 minutes. Stir in lentils and spinach. Whisk whole eggs and egg whites, pour into the pan, and fold gently until just cooked through. The eggs should be softly scrambled throughout the lentils — rich and saucy, not dry.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Harissa Chicken + Sweet Potato + Chickpeas
meals.instructions: Rub chicken with harissa, cumin, and paprika. Roast sweet potato cubes at 400°F for 25 minutes until caramelized. Sear chicken in a hot pan 4–5 minutes per side until cooked through. Whisk tahini with lemon juice, garlic, and water until pourable. Plate chickpeas and sweet potato, top with sliced chicken, and drizzle tahini generously over the bowl.

meals.label: Meal 3
meals.name: Miso Shrimp + Broccoli + Edamame + Cauliflower
meals.instructions: Roast broccoli at 425°F for 18 minutes until edges are crispy. Whisk miso, coconut aminos, sesame oil, ginger, and garlic into a sauce. Sauté cauliflower rice until fluffy. Toss shrimp and edamame in the miso sauce and warm in a skillet for 2 minutes. Serve over cauliflower rice with roasted broccoli alongside.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Turkey + Avocado + Romaine
meals.instructions: Layer turkey slices and sliced avocado in romaine leaves. Add hot sauce to taste.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Sesame Ginger Chicken + Snap Peas + Edamame + Lentils
meals.instructions: Whisk miso, coconut aminos, sesame oil, ginger, and garlic into a sauce. Use half to marinate chicken for 10 minutes. Sear chicken in a hot pan 4–5 minutes per side until cooked through. Blanch snap peas for 1 minute. Toss edamame and snap peas with remaining sauce. Serve chicken sliced over lentils with the dressed vegetables alongside and scallion on top.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Mexican Turkey + Black Beans + Guac + Cauliflower
meals.instructions: Brown turkey with cumin, chili powder, and jalapeño. Stir in black beans and salsa roja and simmer for 2 minutes. Sauté cauliflower rice separately with a pinch of salt. Build the bowl with cauliflower rice as the base, spoon turkey and beans on top, then add a generous scoop of guacamole and a squeeze of lime. Finish with fresh cilantro.

meals.label: Meal 3
meals.name: Mini Tuna Niçoise Bowl
meals.instructions: Whisk Dijon, lemon juice, garlic, and capers into a sharp vinaigrette. Drain and flake tuna into a bowl. Arrange chickpeas, snap peas, cucumber, and olives around the tuna. Spoon vinaigrette over everything and finish with fresh herbs.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Shrimp Cocktail + Avocado + Celery
meals.instructions: Arrange chilled shrimp with cocktail sauce for dipping. Serve with sliced avocado and celery sticks on the side.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Poke-Lite Bowl: Salmon + Tuna + Edamame
meals.instructions: Whisk gochujang, coconut aminos, and sesame oil into a sauce. Sear salmon in a hot pan for 2–3 minutes per side, then flake into chunks. Toss tuna and salmon together with half the sauce. Build the bowl over brown rice with edamame, sliced cucumber, and avocado. Spoon the dressed fish on top and finish with remaining sauce, scallion, sesame seeds, and pickled ginger.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: BBQ Turkey + Sweet Potato + Broccoli
meals.instructions: Roast sweet potato cubes and broccoli at 400°F for 20–25 minutes. Brush turkey with BBQ sauce and roast or broil until glazed and cooked through. Plate sweet potato and broccoli, top with sliced turkey, and finish with sliced avocado.

meals.label: Meal 3
meals.name: Egg + Egg White + Lentil Indian Bowl
meals.instructions: Sauté onion, garlic, ginger, and cumin. Add curry paste and diced tomato and cook for 2 minutes. Stir in lentils and spinach. Whisk the whole egg and egg whites, pour into the pan, and fold gently until just set and creamy throughout.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Turkey + Hummus + Bell Pepper
meals.instructions: Slice bell pepper into strips. Serve with hummus for dipping and turkey slices on the side.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Thai Chicken Larb + Cabbage + Snap Peas

ingredients.name: Lemongrass, shallot, mint, chili, toasted rice (GF)

meals.instructions: Finely chop chicken and cook in a dry pan over high heat until just cooked through. Remove from heat and toss immediately with fish sauce, coconut aminos, lime juice, lemongrass, shallot, and chili. Stir in lentils and most of the mint. Serve over shredded cabbage and snap peas. Finish with remaining fresh mint and toasted rice for crunch.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Shrimp Taco Bowl + Black Beans + Guac
meals.instructions: Season shrimp with cumin, paprika, and jalapeño. Sear in a hot pan 1–2 minutes per side until lightly charred. Sauté cauliflower rice with salt until fluffy. Build the bowl with cauliflower rice and black beans, top with shrimp, spoon salsa roja and guacamole over the top, and finish with lime and cilantro.

meals.label: Meal 3
meals.name: Tuna + Lentils + Chimichurri + Bell Pepper
meals.instructions: Roast bell pepper strips at 425°F until blistered and sweet. Drain and flake tuna. Build the bowl over lentils with spinach and roasted peppers. Top with tuna and spoon chimichurri generously over everything. Finish with a squeeze of lime.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Egg + Egg White + Avocado Bowl
meals.instructions: Scramble whole egg and egg whites with wilted spinach until just set. Serve over sliced avocado and top with hot sauce.

meals.label: Meal 1
meals.name: Chicken Souvlaki + Chickpeas + Dairy-Free Tzatziki
meals.instructions: Marinate chicken with lemon juice, oregano, garlic, thyme, and paprika for 15 minutes. Sear in a hot pan or grill until golden and cooked through. Mix coconut yogurt with dill, minced garlic, and grated cucumber to make the dairy-free tzatziki. Sauté cauliflower rice until fluffy. Build the bowl with cauliflower rice, chickpeas, and sliced chicken, then spoon tzatziki generously over the top.

meals.label: Meal 2
meals.name: Harissa Salmon + Lentils + Broccoli + Greens
meals.instructions: Spread harissa paste over salmon and roast at 400°F for 12–14 minutes until flaky and caramelized at the edges. Roast broccoli alongside until charred. Whisk tahini with lemon juice, garlic, and water until smooth and pourable. Wilt spinach in the warm pan. Plate lentils with spinach and broccoli, lay salmon on top, and drizzle tahini over everything.

meals.label: Meal 3
meals.name: Turkey + Black Beans + Quinoa Bowl
meals.instructions: Season turkey with cumin and paprika and roast until cooked through. Build the bowl over quinoa and black beans with fresh spinach. Slice turkey and lay on top. Spoon salsa roja over the turkey, add sliced avocado, and finish with lime juice and cilantro.

meals.label: Snack
meals.name: Shrimp + Butter Lettuce + Avocado + Lime
meals.instructions: Toss chilled shrimp with lime juice, hot sauce, garlic, and cilantro. Spoon sliced avocado into butter lettuce cups and top with the dressed shrimp.

groceryLists.title: Week 1 — Days 1–7
groceryLists.subtitle: Standard Phase · 2 meals + 1 snack per day

items.text: Shredded cabbage or ½ head green cabbage
Coconut water — ~3.5 liters (about 3 large cartons, for 14 cocktails)
Coconut milk — 1 can (you only need 1–2 tbsp per cocktail)
Cream of tartar — 1 small container (lasts all month)
Collagen peptides — 1 tub (~14 scoops this week)
Canned tuna in water — 4–5 cans (5 oz each)
Black beans, canned — two 15 oz cans
Green lentils, dried — ½ cup dry (or 1 can)
Red lentils, dried — ½ cup dry
GF Thai chili paste — 1 small jar

groceryLists.title: Week 2 — Days 8–14
groceryLists.subtitle: Standard Phase · 2 meals + 1 snack per day

items.text: Coconut water — ~3.5 liters (about 3 large cartons, for 14 cocktails)
Coconut milk — check if any left from Wk 1, restock if needed
Cream of tartar — check if any left from Wk 1
Collagen peptides — ~14 scoops this week (check tub)
Canned tuna in water — 3–4 cans (5 oz each)
Salmon fillet, raw — 2 small (7 oz total)
Green lentils, dried — 1.25 cups dry (or 3 cans)
Red lentils, dried — ¼ cup dry
Black beans, canned — one 15 oz can
GF tikka paste — check if any left from Wk 1
Harissa paste (GF) — check if any left from Wk 1
GF miso paste — check if any left
Coconut aminos — check if any left
Sesame oil — check if any left
Dijon mustard (GF) — check if any left
Kalamata olives — check if any left
GF fish sauce — check if any left

groceryLists.title: Week 3 — Days 15–21
groceryLists.subtitle: Standard Phase · 2 meals + 1 snack per day

items.text: Baby spinach — 2 large bags (5 oz each)
Bell peppers — 4 medium (2 for stuffing, 2 for eating)
Coconut water — ~3.5 liters (about 3 large cartons, for 14 cocktails)
Coconut milk — check if any left from Wk 1–2, restock if needed
Cream of tartar — check if any left from Wk 1
Collagen peptides — ~14 scoops this week (check tub)
Canned tuna in water — 4 cans (5 oz each)
Red lentils, dried — ¾ cup dry
Black beans, canned — one 15 oz can
Brown rice — check if any left from Wk 1–2
Unsweetened coconut yogurt — 1 small container (dairy-free tzatziki)
GF tamarind paste — check if any left from Wk 2
GF BBQ sauce — check if any left
Baharat spice blend (GF) — 1 packet or make from pantry
All Week 1 sauces — check and restock anything low

groceryLists.title: Week 4 — Days 22–30
groceryLists.subtitle: Full Luteal Phase · 3 meals + 1 snack per day · 9 days

items.text: Baby spinach — 2 large bags (5 oz each)
Coconut water — ~4.5 liters (about 4 large cartons, for 18 cocktails)
Coconut milk — check if any left, restock if needed
Cream of tartar — check if any left
Collagen peptides — ~18 scoops this week, restock if needed
Canned tuna in water — 5 cans (5 oz each)
Turkey breast, deli-sliced — 1 lb (lots of snacks this week)
Salmon fillet, raw — 2 fillets (~12 oz total)
Green lentils, dried — ¾ cup dry
Black beans, canned — two 15 oz cans
Edamame, shelled & frozen — 1 large bag (16 oz)
GF BBQ sauce — check if any left
All pantry staples — check and restock anything low

howToUse.title: How to Use This Guide
howToUse.intro: This plan is designed to work with your body's natural hormonal rhythm, not against it. It follows the framework popularized by Dr. Mindy Pelz in her work on fasting and the female cycle.
howToUse.startingInfo: Day 1 = the first day of your period. Figure out approximately where you are in your cycle right now and start on that day. For example, if you're on day 14 of your cycle, start at Day 14 in this plan. Then, when your next period begins, restart the plan from Day 1. From that point on, you'll be fully synced.

phases.label: Phase 1 · Days 1–20
phases.title: Standard Phase
phases.description: During menstruation and the follicular phase, your body responds really well to intermittent fasting. Your estrogen is rising, insulin sensitivity is higher, and your metabolism is running efficiently. You're in your fat-burning sweet spot. You'll eat 2 meals + 1 snack + 2 adrenal cocktails per day, keeping your eating window to roughly 8 hours.

phases.label: Phase 2 · Days 21–30
phases.title: Luteal Phase
phases.description: As progesterone rises in the second half of your cycle, so does your hunger — and that's completely normal. Your body is preparing for the possibility of pregnancy and needs more consistent fuel. Fighting this hunger leads to cravings, bingeing, and falling off track. Instead, we lean into it: you'll eat 3 meals + 1 snack + 2 adrenal cocktails per day. More frequent eating keeps blood sugar stable and progesterone supported.

howToUse.callout: Most diet plans ignore your cycle entirely — which is why so many women feel like they "fail" every month around the same time. You're not failing. You're fighting your biology. This plan works with it instead.

understandingCalories.title: Understanding Your Calories
understandingCalories.intro: This plan is built around approximately 1,500 calories per day. That's a caloric deficit for most women — meaning you'll be eating less than your body burns, which is what drives fat loss.

sections.heading: This is not a forever number
sections.text: 1,500 calories is a tool for fat loss, not a lifestyle. Once you reach your goals, you should increase to your maintenance calories. Think of this as a phase, not a permanent way of eating.

sections.heading: If your deficit target is higher than 1,500 cal
sections.text: Use this plan as a base and add to it. Extra protein, more vegetables, or slightly larger portions are all good options. Don't skip meals just to stay at 1,500 if your body needs more.

sections.heading: If your deficit target is lower than 1,500 cal
sections.text: Be careful here. Going too low can hurt your metabolism, your hormones, and your energy levels. For most women, 1,200 calories should be the absolute floor — and even that should only be temporary. Please don't push lower than that.

understandingCalories.callout: Use your TDEE as your guide. This plan gives you the structure — your numbers tell you whether to follow it as written or adjust the portions to fit your body.
understandingCalories.bodyText:
How to Find Your Number

Search for a TDEE (Total Daily Energy Expenditure) calculator online. Enter your height, weight, age, and activity level. That number is roughly what your body burns each day at your current size.

To lose about 1 pound per week, subtract 500 calories from your TDEE. That's your personal deficit target — and for many women, it lands right around 1,500 calories, which is why this plan uses that as the starting point.

whyProteinFiber.title: Why High Protein + High Fiber
whyProteinFiber.intro: Every meal in this plan is built around two pillars: plenty of protein and plenty of fiber. Here's why both matter so much when you're eating in a deficit.
proteinText: When you eat in a caloric deficit, your body doesn't just burn fat — it can break down muscle too. That's the opposite of what you want. Losing muscle slows your metabolism, changes how your body looks, and makes it harder to keep the weight off.

Eating high protein tells your body to preserve muscle and burn fat instead. This is critical for your metabolism (muscle burns more calories than fat, even at rest) and for how you look and feel as you lose weight. Every meal in this plan is built around a protein-rich foundation.

fiberReasons.heading: Keeps you full
fiberReasons.text: When you're eating fewer calories, hunger is the number one reason people quit. Fiber slows digestion and keeps you feeling satisfied for hours. This is arguably the most important benefit when you're in a deficit.

fiberReasons.heading: Supports your hormones
fiberReasons.text: Fiber helps your body process and eliminate excess estrogen. When estrogen builds up, it can cause bloating, mood swings, and weight gain — especially around the midsection.

fiberReasons.heading: Feeds your gut
fiberReasons.text: A healthy gut microbiome affects everything from your mood to your metabolism. Fiber feeds the beneficial bacteria that keep your digestive system running smoothly.

whyProteinFiber.fiberIntro: Fiber is the unsung hero of fat loss. Most people don't talk about it enough. Here's what it actually does for you:

adrenalCocktail.title: Your Adrenal Cocktail
adrenalCocktail.intro: You'll see two adrenal cocktails built into every day of this plan. Here's everything you need to know about what they are, why they matter, and how to make them.

sections.heading: What Is It?
sections.text: A simple, nourishing drink designed to support your adrenal glands — the tiny powerhouses responsible for your stress response, energy levels, and hormonal balance.

sections.heading: Why It Matters
sections.text: When you're in a caloric deficit, your body can interpret the reduced food intake as a form of stress. Your adrenal glands may start working overtime, which can lead to fatigue, cravings, and hormonal disruption. The adrenal cocktail provides a targeted blend of minerals and nutrients to keep them supported so that doesn't happen.

sections.heading: When to Drink It
sections.text: One at 10:00 AM and one at 2:00 PM. These are the times your cortisol naturally dips during the day — and the cocktail helps smooth out those energy valleys so you don't hit a wall.

recipe.label: The Recipe · Makes 1 Cocktail
recipe.title: Adrenal Cocktail
ingredients: 2 tbsp freshly squeezed orange juice (about half an orange)
recipe.macrosNote: ~160 cal · 4g protein per cocktail · 2 per day = 320 cal / 8g protein total Already included in your daily calorie and protein totals.
